title: Death rate from neonatal preterm birth complications
subtitle: The estimated death rate from [neonatal](#dod:neonatal) [preterm birth complications](#dod:preterm-birth-complications) in children under five years old, per 100,000.
caption:
Death rate from neonatal preterm birth complications

The estimated death rate from neonatal preterm birth complications in children under five years old, per 100,000.

This choropleth map displays the estimated death rate from neonatal preterm birth complications in children under five years old per 100,000 for the year 2023. The data is visualized using a color scale ranging from pale yellow for the lowest rates to dark blue for the highest rates, with specific thresholds at 0, 10, 20, 50, 100, 200, 500, and 1,000. The highest mortality rates, represented by the darkest blue shades indicating values between 200 and 1,000 per 100,000, are concentrated primarily across the African continent, including nations such as Nigeria, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and Somalia, as well as Yemen in the Middle East and Haiti in the Caribbean. A second tier of elevated rates, falling roughly between 50 and 200 per 100,000 and shown in medium blue tones, covers much of South Asia, including India and Pakistan, alongside parts of Southeast Asia like Indonesia and Papua New Guinea, and several countries in South America such as Bolivia and Peru. In contrast, the lowest death rates, depicted in pale yellow and light green corresponding to values between 0 and 20 per 100,000, are visible across most of Europe, including the United Kingdom, France, and Germany, as well as in Canada, the United States, Australia, and Russia. Greenland is marked with a hatched pattern indicating no data is available for that region. The visual distribution highlights a distinct geographic disparity, with the most severe burdens located in sub-Saharan Africa and parts of Asia, while North America, Europe, and Oceania generally exhibit significantly lower rates.
